comment.body:
Intraday Game Plan – SPX | Wed 30 Jul
	•	Center of Gravity 6375
Opening action around this level tells the tale: above it, bulls control the pace; below it, bears press for mean-reversion.
	•	Bullish Playbook (spot ≥ 6375)
	1.	6380 is the first hurdle — Call-Wall + Upside Pivot.
	2.	A clean break and hold over 6380 often triggers dealer chasing up to the Upside Target 6400.
• Beyond 6400 positive gamma fades, so momentum usually stalls.
	•	Bearish Playbook (failure under 6375)
	1.	Price is magnetized toward Put Support 6370; weak defense turns it into a stepping-stone lower.
	2.	Sustainable trade under 6370 sharpens sell pressure toward the Downside Target 6350 where liquidity is thicker and snap-back rallies are common.
	•	Projected Day-Range (± 0.74 % from 6370) → 6323 – 6417
Watch for exhaustion or heavy absorption as we approach those extremes.
	•	Tactical Notes
	•	Long scalps only if 6380 flips into support after shallow pull-backs (< 10 pts).
	•	Tactical shorts on the first decisive rejection of 6380, trimming size into 6370 → 6350.
	•	Avoid chasing upside through 6400; gamma neutrality there often breeds slow grind or stall.

comment.body:
No rate cuts is priced in however as Alma wrote in today morning's note, the tone of the conference and how Jpow positions this will be keenly observed by the market.  Will probably flip on a dime and better to wait until Jpow's conference is done today. Also earnings will be keenly watched by the market partcipants. 

"If Powell doesn’t ease on “tariff inflation risk/uncertainity” narrative, the market will sell, and that will hurt Trump. As explained, they need lower rates to manage debt burden somehow."

"Microsoft, Meta, Amazon and Apple will report earnings today and tomorrow, the expectations are “solid revenue growth”"

comment.body: If we are being specific, you will not get guidance from the 2:30pm PT presser today. Dot plot will only be revealed when fed meeting minutes come out 3 weeks later. Jpow has not had the habit of commenting on what happens in a future meeting and has always said will depend on the data.

comment.body: Watch what they say though if they put emphasis on inflation that means that it’s not transient. He used this in May and stopped talking about it (remember 2021?). That should tell you if there is a fed pivot even if they come out with “data dependent”. It’s likely why we haven’t sold off on CPI prints
